title: alchemilla vulgaris var. xanthochlora
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. xanthochlora, also known as the yellow lady's mantle, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is used in herbal medicine due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla vulgaris var. xanthochlora are believed to offer several health benefits, including wound healing, anti-oxidative effects, and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and menstrual cramps. Alchemilla vulgaris var. xanthochlora can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
